metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

coverage.verify_error: step 'review@then/verify_branch@then/verify' failed: the LLM provider call failed: Streamed response ended without content or tool calls

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command (or deterministic shell check) for each AC — e.g., `rebar explain CRIT-1 | grep -q 'affirmative rule'` for AC4 and `rebar review --help | grep -q 'docs/authoring'` for AC3.
findings.finding: No proving command is specified for any acceptance criterion; an executing agent cannot confirm done-ness without guessing what command to run.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all five items)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether the drift-parity CI gate already covers the new authoring guide or must be extended, and add a step (with a proving command) that confirms the gate fires on a deliberate guide-vs-registry divergence.
findings.finding: AC2's 'CI-parity-gated' claim relies on 'the existing prompt/routing drift-parity gate' without specifying whether that gate already covers the new authoring guide or needs to be extended — an executor cannot know what CI work is in scope.
findings.location: What section ('kept in sync by the existing prompt/routing drift-parity gate'); AC2

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace AC5 with an observable outcome (e.g., 'all criterion anchor IDs in the authoring guide match IDs present in the final WS1-8 criteria registry') or demote it to a sequencing note in the plan body.
findings.finding: AC5 ('Sequenced after WS1-8 — do not anchor rubrics about to be rewritten') is a process constraint, not an observable outcome; it cannot be evaluated as true-or-false after this work is done.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, fifth item
findings.suggested_fix: Convert to a plan-body note: 'Start only after WS1-8 criteria IDs are stable; the authoring guide's anchor IDs must match the committed registry (verified by a CI diff check).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Break AC4 into explicit per-surface sub-criteria (CLI, MCP, library) or explicitly state whether all three are required in the same session vs. sequenced, and add a proving command per surface.
findings.finding: 'rebar explain <criterion-id> in CLI + MCP + library' bundles three potentially independent implementation surfaces into a single AC with no breakdown, making it ambiguous whether all three must ship in the same session and whether partial delivery (e.g., CLI only) is acceptable.
findings.location: AC4: 'rebar explain <criterion-id> in CLI + MCP + library'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify an anchor-stability mechanism for criterion deep-links (e.g., explicit `<a id="{criterion-id}">` anchors in the generated guide + a CI check that verifies each criterion-id in the registry resolves to a live anchor) so coach output links don't silently break on doc edits.
findings.finding: The anchored deep-links emitted by coach output create a cross-component contract between the coach formatter and the authoring-guide Markdown, but the plan specifies no mechanism to guarantee anchor stability (e.g., explicit HTML anchor IDs, a CI check that verifies each criterion-id maps to a live anchor in the guide).
findings.location: What / Discoverability bullet: 'coach output cites an ANCHORED deep-link to the criterion section'
findings.suggested_fix: Generate explicit HTML anchor tags keyed by criterion-id (e.g., ``) in the authoring guide rather than relying on heading-text slugs; extend the existing parity gate to assert that every registry criterion-id has a corresponding anchor in the guide.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify a single shared `explain(criterion_id)` function/module in the library layer that the CLI and MCP surfaces delegate to, so registry-lookup logic is implemented once and the three-component coupling is mediated by an explicit interface.
findings.finding: The `rebar explain <criterion-id>` command is to be delivered across three components (CLI, MCP, library) with no specified shared interface boundary, silently introducing three-way coupling to the criteria registry lookup logic.
findings.location: What / Discoverability bullet: 'rebar explain <criterion-id> command across CLI/MCP/library'
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the plan: 'The library exposes an `explain(criterion_id) -> CriterionDoc` function; CLI and MCP are thin wrappers over this call.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify non-happy-path behavior for `rebar explain`: define the output/exit-code for an unknown criterion-id (e.g., fuzzy suggestion + non-zero exit), a missing/malformed registry (fail with actionable message), and a criterion whose authoring-guide section hasn't been generated yet (fallback to registry definition).
findings.finding: The `rebar explain <criterion-id>` command (new CLI/MCP/library surface) specifies only the happy path (criterion found → formatted output). No error states are described: unknown criterion-id, malformed registry, unavailable backing file, or empty criterion body.
findings.location: AC: `rebar explain <criterion-id> in CLI + MCP + library`
findings.suggested_fix: Add an explicit error contract subsection: (1) unknown id → print 'Unknown criterion: <id>. Run `rebar explain --list` to see valid ids.' + exit 1; (2) registry unavailable → 'Cannot load criterion registry: <path>. Re-run after `rebar build`.' + exit 2; (3) guide section missing → fall back to the raw registry description with a note that the authoring guide for this criterion is pending.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Acknowledge that adding anchored deep-links changes the coach output format, and note which existing tests/consumers (snapshot tests, MCP parsers, downstream CI jobs) need updating or a compatibility shim.
findings.finding: The plan adds anchored deep-links to existing coach output (changing its format), but does not acknowledge downstream callers — tests, parsers, or CI jobs — that may depend on the current coach output structure.
findings.location: What / R-5 section: 'coach output cites an ANCHORED deep-link to the criterion section'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Consider splitting R-3 (asymmetric-error invariant doc, single markdown file, no sequencing dependency) from R-5 (authoring guide + rebar explain tooling, requires WS1-8 completion) into separate child tickets since they have different actors, different release readiness, and independent value.
findings.finding: R-3 (asymmetric-error invariant doc targeting rubric tuners/maintainers) and R-5 (authoring guide + rebar explain tooling targeting plan authors) are distinct outcomes with different actors and independent release value, yet are bundled into one WS without decomposition into children.
findings.location: ## What / ## Acceptance Criteria — R-3 vs R-5 split
findings.suggested_fix: Create WS10a (R-3 only): write docs/plan-review-gate.md + checklist note — no sequencing dependency, ships immediately. Create WS10b (R-5): authoring guide + rebar explain + deep-links — sequenced after WS1-8.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a testing plan for `rebar explain <criterion-id>`: specify happy-path (known id returns guide section + anchored URL), failure-path (unknown/malformed id returns actionable error, not panic/empty), and a cross-surface consistency assertion (CLI/MCP/library return equivalent content for the same id).
findings.finding: The `rebar explain <criterion-id>` command is a new user-facing feature across three surfaces (CLI, MCP, library) with no testing plan — no happy-path, no failure-path for unknown/malformed criterion IDs, and no contract test for surface consistency.
findings.location: ## Acceptance Criteria — 'rebar explain <criterion-id> in CLI + MCP + library'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: 'rebar explain <unknown-id> returns a non-zero exit code and a "criterion not found" message listing available ids; rebar explain <known-id> output in CLI, MCP tool response, and library return value all contain the same criterion title and deep-link URL (cross-surface parity test).'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'existing prompt/routing drift-parity gate' with an explicit description of the NEW CI check needed to enforce that the authoring guide Markdown contains a section for every criterion in CANONICAL_LLM (the existing gate only checks criteria_routing.json, not Markdown content).
findings.finding: The plan asserts the Markdown authoring guide will be 'kept in sync by the existing prompt/routing drift-parity gate,' but the existing gate (`python -m rebar.llm.plan_review.registry validate-routing`) only checks that `criteria_routing.json` entries match `CANONICAL_LLM` — it has no capability to detect drift between a Markdown doc and the criteria registry. No such sync mechanism exists for Markdown content.
findings.location: What section, R-5: 'kept in sync by the existing prompt/routing drift-parity gate'; AC: 'Registry-derived authoring guide (longer-form Markdown, section-keyed), CI-parity-gated'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC for a new script (e.g. `check_authoring_guide_coverage.py`) that asserts every criterion id in `registry.CANONICAL_LLM` has a corresponding `## <criterion-id>` anchor section in the authoring guide, and wire it into the same CI job as `validate-routing`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the specific files requiring edits for `rebar explain`: at minimum `src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py` (dispatch intercept), `src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py` (implementation), `src/rebar/_mcp_llm.py` or `src/rebar/_mcp_reads.py` (MCP tool), and `src/rebar/llm/__init__.py` (library export), and classify this as new-behavior CREATION.
findings.finding: `rebar explain <criterion-id>` does not exist anywhere in the codebase (no CLI arm, no MCP tool, no library export). It is pure CREATION (new behavior). The plan names it as a deliverable across CLI, MCP, and library surfaces but does not enumerate the specific files that must be edited to wire it in.
findings.location: What section / AC: 'rebar explain <criterion-id> command across CLI/MCP/library'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Files changed' table to the What section listing these four files, analogous to how the existing `rebar criteria eval` command is wired (see src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py:463 + src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py:419 for the pattern).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name `src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py` (`_review_plan` function, `argparse.ArgumentParser` call) as the edit target for the `--help epilog` URL addition.
findings.finding: The `--help epilog` URL addition for `rebar review-plan` targets `src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py:_review_plan()` (an existing file), but this file is not named in the plan. The current `argparse.ArgumentParser` call at line 303 has no `epilog=` argument; adding one is a concrete edit to an existing file that is unenumerated.
findings.location: What section / AC: 'Discoverability: full URL in review-plan --help epilog'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py` (P1Finding/coaching model) and `src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py` (`_render_plan_review_text`) as edit targets for the anchored deep-link feature, and decide whether the link is a new verdict field (requiring `src/rebar/schemas/plan_review_verdict.schema.json` update) or embedded in an existing field.
findings.finding: The coach output deep-link feature requires modifying the existing coaching formatter at `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py` and/or the CLI text renderer at `src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py:_render_plan_review_text()`, but neither file is named in the plan. These are real existing consumers of the criteria data that will need updating.
findings.location: What section: 'coach output cites an ANCHORED deep-link to the criterion section'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'existing prompt/routing drift-parity gate' with a NEW CI step (e.g., a script that checks every criterion id in `CANONICAL_LLM` ∪ activated project ids has a correspondingly-headed section in the authoring guide) and update the AC to specify what that gate checks.
findings.finding: The plan states the authoring guide is 'kept in sync by the existing prompt/routing drift-parity gate,' but the existing gate (validate_packaged_routing in registry.py + check_prompt_parity in prompts.py) only validates structural schema properties of criteria_routing.json and prompt template variable sets. It cannot detect drift between a new prose Markdown authoring guide and the registry content. A new CI step must be built; the existing gate is not sufficient for this purpose.
findings.location: What / R-5 bullet: 'kept in sync by the existing prompt/routing drift-parity gate'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: 'A CI check verifies every criterion id in the effective registry has a section anchor in the authoring guide (e.g., ## {id} or a YAML front-matter id list); the check is added to the existing validate-routing Makefile target or a new rebar guide-check command.' The prose-quality of examples cannot be auto-generated, but section-presence is machine-checkable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify concretely how the CI parity gate will cover the authoring guide's content (e.g. a new gate that checks every CANONICAL_LLM criterion id has a matching `## <id>` section in the authoring guide, or a generation step that renders the guide from the same registry source), rather than asserting the existing routing-structure gate covers it.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the authoring guide will be 'kept in sync by the existing prompt/routing drift-parity gate,' but the existing gate (registry.py `validate_packaged_routing`) only validates `criteria_routing.json` against `CANONICAL_LLM` vocabulary — it does not check Markdown authoring-guide content. This is an unverified capability assertion about the scope of an existing mechanism.
findings.location: What / R-5: 'CI-parity-gated' (AC: 'Registry-derived authoring guide (longer-form Markdown, section-keyed), CI-parity-gated')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a dedicated CI check (e.g. a lightweight script or test) that asserts: for each criterion id in `CANONICAL_LLM`, the authoring guide contains a section anchored to that id. This is distinct from `validate_packaged_routing` and can reuse the same `CANONICAL_LLM` set.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief feasibility note (or a scoped spike) for `rebar explain <criterion-id>` covering: (a) which CLI dispatch pattern it follows (intercept like `review-plan` vs golden-pinned help arm), (b) whether it belongs in `_mcp_reads` or `_mcp_llm`, and (c) what the output schema looks like — before committing to CLI+MCP+library simultaneously.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `rebar explain <criterion-id>` as a new CLI subcommand, MCP tool, and library function simultaneously, but no such command, tool registration, or library entry exists anywhere in the codebase; this is an entirely new surface with no prior art in the rebar CLI dispatch infrastructure.
findings.location: What / R-5 bullet: 'rebar explain <criterion-id> command across CLI/MCP/library (rustc --explain / ruff rule pattern)'
findings.suggested_fix: Model `rebar explain F1` after `rebar criteria eval <id>` for the CLI intercept pattern (no golden pin, owns its `--help` via argparse), place it in `_mcp_reads` as a pure doc-lookup tool (no `REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_LLM` gate), and add it as a library export `rebar.llm.explain_criterion(id)` returning `{id, name, scenario, checklist, guide_url}`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify or correct the sync mechanism for the authoring guide: either name the specific new CI check that will enforce guide↔registry parity (e.g. a script that diffs guide section anchors against `CANONICAL_LLM`), or replace 'existing … gate' with 'a new parity gate added in this story.'
findings.finding: The plan claims the authoring guide will be 'kept in sync by the existing prompt/routing drift-parity gate,' but the existing parity gate (`validate_packaged_routing` in `registry.py`) only checks that `criteria_routing.json` JSON entries match `CANONICAL_LLM` — it has no mechanism to verify that a Markdown file's section headings (or content) match the criteria registry.
findings.location: What / R-5 bullet: 'kept in sync by the existing prompt/routing drift-parity gate'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the output schema for `rebar explain <criterion-id>` (fields: at minimum criterion id, name, rubric body, checklist items; JSON + text modes), its error contract for unknown/unavailable criterion ids, and its MCP annotation tier (READ_ONLY vs LLM-gated) so all three surfaces share one contract.
findings.finding: `rebar explain <criterion-id>` is a net-new CLI/MCP/library command with no implementation in the codebase and no output schema specified in the plan. The plan names it as a deliverable across three surfaces (CLI + MCP + library) but never defines: the output format/schema (what fields does it return?), error handling for an unknown criterion id, error handling when the registry is unavailable, or how the MCP tool is registered (read-only vs LLM-gated). An executor implementing this command has no contract to conform to.
findings.location: What / R-5 bullet: 'rebar explain <criterion-id> command across CLI/MCP/library'; AC: 'rebar explain <criterion-id> in CLI + MCP + library'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC or sub-spec: 'rebar explain <criterion-id> returns {id, name, description, checklist[], guide_url} as JSON (--output json) or formatted text (--output text); exits non-zero with a clear error message on unknown criterion id or missing registry; MCP tool is READ_ONLY (no LLM call, reads only from the packaged registry).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the deep-link construction protocol for coach output: define the base URL (config key or hardcoded docs path), the anchor format (e.g. `#{criterion-id}-{slugified-name}`), where the URL is injected in the render path (a new `{criterion_url}` slot in the coach template, a renderer post-processing step, or a config-driven prefix), and the fallback when the guide URL is not configured (omit link, emit criterion id only).
findings.finding: The anchored deep-link emitted by coach output is a new cross-component contract between the Pass-4 coach renderer and the authoring-guide Markdown file, but the plan specifies no protocol for how the link is constructed. The existing coach rendering path (`pass4_coach` → `render_coach_notes` in `review_kernel/coach.py`) renders from `MOVE_REGISTRY` templates using only a `{subject}` placeholder — there is no URL/anchor generation step. The plan says 'coach output cites an ANCHORED deep-link to the criterion section' but never defines: the base URL, the anchor format (e.g. `#t8-llm-prompt-structural-completeness`), who owns the URL (coach prompt? renderer? config?), or what happens if the guide URL is unconfigured.
findings.location: What / R-5 bullet: 'coach output cites an ANCHORED deep-link to the criterion section (ESLint/Ruff/rustc pattern)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the concrete sync mechanism for the authoring guide: either (a) a generation script (`rebar generate-authoring-guide`) whose output is committed and whose CI check is a regenerate-and-diff, or (b) a new parity gate that reads both the effective criteria set and the guide's section anchors and fails CI when they diverge — and add it to the existing `validate_packaged_routing` CI call or a new CI step.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the authoring guide will be 'kept in sync by the existing prompt/routing drift-parity gate,' but the confirmed existing gate (`validate_packaged_routing` in `registry.py`) only validates routing JSON ↔ CANONICAL_LLM vocabulary sync, and `check_prompt_parity` in `prompts.py` only validates prompt variable declarations. Neither gate reads or validates a Markdown authoring guide file. There is no defined mechanism — no CI check, no generation script, no validation rule — that would fail when a criterion is added/renamed but the authoring guide section is not updated.
findings.location: What / R-5 bullet: 'kept in sync by the existing prompt/routing drift-parity gate'; AC: 'Registry-derived authoring guide (longer-form Markdown, section-keyed), CI-parity-gated'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
